events.title: Mystery oil slick reported offshore near East Flower Garden Bank (Texas coast)
events.description: A light-brown oil slick was reported offshore south of East Flower Garden Bank National Marine Sanctuary. A Coast Guard overflight later observed a smaller, patchy slick, and no further response was considered warranted due to the distance offshore and the small amount of product.

location.name: Off the Texas coast (near East Flower Garden Bank National Marine Sanctuary), USA

sources.headline: Texas Coast Mystery Slick (oil) reported south of East Flower Garden Bank National Marine Sanctuary

key_quotes.quote: a thick light-brown oil slick was reported offshore
Coast Guard overflight located a smaller patchy slick

events.title: Offshore pipeline leak on Pelto Number 1 lease reported near Whiskey Pass (Louisiana)
events.description: A pipeline leak on the Pelto Number 1 oil lease was reported, with a brown crude oil slick observed offshore west of the Mississippi Delta near Whiskey Pass, Louisiana. The incident record describes discovery/notification of the leak and the presence of crude oil on the water.

location.name: Offshore of Whiskey Pass, Louisiana, USA

sources.headline: Pelto Number 1 offshore pipeline leak creates brown crude oil slick near Whiskey Pass, Louisiana

key_quotes.quote: A pipeline leak ... was discovered
a brown crude oil slick reported offshore

events.title: Diesel spill reported in Atchafalaya Channel near Point Au Far Island (Louisiana)
events.description: A diesel spill estimated at roughly 300–400 gallons was discovered in the Atchafalaya Channel near Point Au Far Island, Louisiana. The report noted rainbow sheen over a wide area of the ship channel.

location.name: Atchafalaya Channel near Point Au Far Island, Louisiana, USA

sources.headline: Diesel spill discovered in Atchafalaya Channel near Point Au Far Island, Louisiana

key_quotes.quote: estimated 300–400 gallon diesel spill was discovered
rainbow sheen reported over a wide area

events.title: Fuel barge Ocean 211 grounded at Horn Pass near Pascagoula (Mississippi) with product at risk
events.description: The barge Ocean 211, carrying fuel products, grounded at Horn Pass near Pascagoula, Mississippi. At the time of reporting, no product was reported leaking, but substantial quantities of gasoline, diesel, and jet fuel were listed as at risk of release.

location.name: Horn Pass near Pascagoula, Mississippi, USA

sources.headline: Barge Ocean 211 grounds at Horn Pass near Pascagoula, Mississippi with large fuel cargo at risk

key_quotes.quote: grounded at Horn Pass near Pascagoula
no product was reported leaking ... quantities ... at risk

events.title: Flash flooding in multiple Ohio counties after heavy rains
events.description: A USGS monthly conditions report describes heavy rains that led to flash flooding and road closures in Coshocton, Meigs, Monroe, and Muskingum Counties in Ohio.

location.name: Ohio, United States (Coshocton, Meigs, Monroe, Muskingum Counties)

key_quotes.quote: Heavy rains caused flash flooding and closed roads in multiple Ohio counties.

events.title: Wabash River crest at Vincennes reported as highest since March 1997
events.description: A USGS monthly conditions report describes early May heavy rains contributing to flooding along the Wabash and White Rivers. It reports the Wabash River crest at Vincennes on May 12 as the highest level since March 1997.

key_quotes.quote: ...the Wabash River crest at Vincennes on May 12 was reported as the highest since March 1997.

events.title: Late-May flooding prompts emergency declaration in Crook County, Oregon
events.description: A USGS monthly conditions report describes heavy rainfall on saturated ground leading to flooding in central Oregon. It reports a state of emergency declaration in Crook County, evacuations in/near Prineville, and water spilling over Ochoco Dam around May 29.

location.name: Crook County, Oregon, United States (Prineville; Ochoco Dam)

key_quotes.quote: ...Crook County... declared a state of emergency... water spilled over Ochoco Dam near Prineville... evacuations...

events.title: May 1998 reported as markedly warmer than normal across the U.S. Northeast
events.description: A Cornell News story referencing a regional climate center report describes May 1998 as significantly warmer than normal across the 12-state U.S. Northeast, noting record-rank warmth for states such as Maine and New York.

key_quotes.quote: ...May 1998 as markedly warmer than normal across the 12-state Northeast...

events.title: Severe thunderstorms and tornadoes with localized flooding in northern Oklahoma
events.description: An NWS event page describes a severe thunderstorm complex producing multiple tornadoes and heavy rainfall in Oklahoma. The account includes localized flooding reports near Cherokee (including at/near a national wildlife refuge) and flooding in Blackwell.

location.name: Oklahoma, United States (northern/central Oklahoma; Cherokee; Blackwell)

key_quotes.quote: ...multiple tornadoes and heavy rain... flooding near Cherokee... flooded streets... in Blackwell...

events.title: Mw 7.5 earthquake southeast of Taiwan near the southwestern Ryukyu Islands
events.description: A moment magnitude 7.5 earthquake was recorded southeast of Taiwan, near the southwestern Ryukyu Islands (around Ishigaki). The sources list the event time in late May 3, 1998 (UTC).

key_quotes.quote: M7.5 Earthquake 1998-05-03 23:30:21 UTC

events.title: Mw 6.6 earthquake in Cochabamba Department, Bolivia (Aiquile/Mizque area)
events.description: A magnitude ~6.6 earthquake was recorded in Bolivia’s Cochabamba Department, near the Aiquile/Mizque area. The draft indicates severe regional impacts, but the provided source link is an index-style listing without corroborating impact details.

key_quotes.quote: 1998-05-22 04:48:50 UTC M6.6 Earthquake

events.title: Mw 6.6 earthquake in northern Afghanistan (Takhar/Badakhshan region)
events.description: A magnitude ~6.6 earthquake was recorded in northern Afghanistan in the Hindu Kush region, near the Takhar/Badakhshan area. The provided source link supports the event time and magnitude; impact details in the draft are not corroborated by additional sources here.

key_quotes.quote: 1998-05-30 06:22:28 UTC M6.6 Earthquake

events.title: M 5.5 earthquake offshore east of Mersa Matruh, Egypt (felt in Cairo)
events.description: A magnitude 5.5 earthquake was recorded offshore in the Mediterranean Sea east of Mersa Matruh, Egypt. The draft notes it was felt in Cairo and mentions an injury, but only the provided listing source is included here.

location.name: Egypt (national proxy; offshore event reported east of Mersa Matruh, felt in Cairo)

key_quotes.quote: 1998-05-28 18:33:28 UTC M5.5 Earthquake

events.title: Smoke from Mexico & Central America fires impacts U.S. Gulf Coast (GOES-8 observed; Texas health alert)
events.description: Satellite imagery documented extensive smoke transport from fires in Mexico and Central America into the U.S. Gulf Coast region. The smoke reduced visibility and prompted public health messaging in Texas about smoke and particulate impacts.

location.name: Austin, Texas, United States (Gulf Coast smoke impacts referenced)

key_quotes.quote: large area of smoke from fires in Mexico and Central America moving northward over the Gulf of Mexico

dedupe.cluster_hint: 1998 Mexico/Central America fires smoke impacts US Gulf Coast

events.title: Mexico and Central America forest-fire crisis with regional haze
events.description: Reports in mid-May 1998 described widespread fires across Mexico and parts of Central America during drought conditions. The resulting smoke and haze degraded air quality and visibility and was reported to spread beyond the burn areas, including toward parts of the United States.

location.name: Mexico City, Mexico (regional fires/haze reported across Mexico and Central America)

sources.headline: Mexico/Central America fires and haze (archive story)

events.title: Hong Kong closed five beaches due to a newly identified red tide
events.description: Hong Kong authorities closed five beaches after they were hit by a newly identified red-tide organism. Reporting also described substantial fish losses since mid-March, indicating broader marine ecosystem impacts associated with a harmful algal bloom.

sources.headline: Government closes five beaches hit by new type of red tide

key_quotes.quote: Government closes five beaches hit by new type of red tide

dedupe.cluster_hint: 1998 Hong Kong red tide beach closures

events.title: Domoic-acid event linked to a Pseudo-nitzschia bloom caused sea lion illness and deaths in California
events.description: A Pseudo-nitzschia harmful algal bloom produced domoic acid that entered the marine food web and was associated with neurological illness and mortality in California sea lions stranded along the central California coast. Sources describe the event as occurring during May–June 1998, affecting animals in areas including the Monterey Bay region.

location.name: Central California coast, USA (Monterey Bay region)

sources.headline: Domoic acid toxicity in California sea lions (Zalophus californianus) stranded along the central California coast, May–October 1998
Domoic acid toxicity in California sea lions (Zalophus californianus) stranded along the central California coast, May–October 1998

key_quotes.quote: Domoic acid toxicity in California sea lions ... stranded along the central California coast

dedupe.cluster_hint: 1998 California sea lion domoic acid event

events.title: Severe coral bleaching in the Maldives caused high coral mortality in late April to mid-May 1998
events.description: Studies reported severe coral bleaching across central atolls of the Maldives from late April through mid-May 1998, coinciding with elevated sea-surface temperatures. The reports described very high mortality in branching corals and major reductions in live coral cover on surveyed reefs.

sources.headline: Coral bleaching in the Maldives in 1998: results of phase II of the coral reef monitoring programme
Coral reef monitoring and the 1998 mass bleaching event in the Maldives (repository record)

key_quotes.quote: Coral bleaching in the Maldives in 1998

events.title: EPA final amendments to HON NESHAP for SOCMI add THBA and crotonaldehyde processes and remove acetaldol
events.description: EPA issued final amendments to the Hazardous Organic NESHAP (HON) for the Synthetic Organic Chemical Manufacturing Industry (SOCMI). The rule adds tetrahydrobenzaldehyde (THBA) and crotonaldehyde processes, removes acetaldol, and sets compliance dates for the added processes.

key_quotes.quote: final amendments to the Hazardous Organic NESHAP (HON) for the Synthetic Organic Chemical Manufacturing Industry (SOCMI)

dedupe.fingerprint: 1998-05-12|epa final amendments hon neshap socmi thba crotonaldehyde acetaldol|us

events.title: U.S. Fish and Wildlife Service final Listing Priority Guidance for FY 1998–1999 under ESA
events.description: The U.S. Fish and Wildlife Service published final Listing Priority Guidance setting relative priorities for Endangered Species Act listing activities for fiscal years 1998 and 1999. The guidance addresses prioritization of emergency listings and other listing-related actions and is effective May 8, 1998.

key_quotes.quote: final guidance setting relative priorities for ESA listing activities for fiscal years 1998 and 1999

dedupe.cluster_hint: FWS ESA Listing Priority Guidance FY1998-1999
dedupe.fingerprint: 1998-05-08|u s fish and wildlife service final listing priority guidance fy 1998 1999 under esa|us

events.title: EPA direct final rule delegates certain NESHAP implementation/enforcement authority to Nevada agencies
events.description: EPA published a direct final rule granting delegation authority to the Nevada Division of Environmental Protection and the Washoe County District Health Department for implementation and enforcement of specified unchanged NESHAP standards under Clean Air Act section 112 in Nevada. The rule also approves the county program for receiving delegation of unchanged NESHAPs for certain non-Title V sources.

key_quotes.quote: direct final rule granting delegation authority to Nevada Division of Environmental Protection and Washoe County District Health Department

dedupe.cluster_hint: EPA NESHAP delegation to Nevada NDEP/WCDHD
dedupe.fingerprint: 1998-05-27|epa direct final rule delegates certain neshap implementation enforcement authority to nevada agencies|us-nv

events.title: EPA direct final rule clarifies/corrects Recycled Used Oil Management Standards
events.description: EPA issued a direct final rule (with a parallel proposed rule) to correct errors and clarify ambiguities in used oil management standards. The action addresses issues including applicability related to PCB-contaminated used oil, certain release response requirements in non-authorized states, mixtures of conditionally exempt small quantity generator wastes and used oil, and recordkeeping for marketers of on-specification used oil.

key_quotes.quote: direct final rule ... to eliminate errors and clarify ambiguities in used oil management standards

dedupe.cluster_hint: EPA used oil management standards corrections/clarifications (direct final)
dedupe.fingerprint: 1998-05-06|epa direct final rule clarifies corrects recycled used oil management standards|us

events.title: EPA proposes RCRA hazardous-waste delisting for certain wastes at Occidental Chemical Ingleside, Texas
events.description: EPA published a proposed rule and request for comment on a petition from Occidental Chemical Corporation to exclude certain solid wastes generated at its Ingleside, Texas facility from RCRA hazardous-waste listings. EPA also sought comment on use of a fate-and-transport model used in its evaluation.

key_quotes.quote: proposed rule and request for comment to grant a petition ... to exclude certain solid wastes ... from RCRA hazardous-waste listings

dedupe.cluster_hint: EPA proposed RCRA delisting Occidental Chemical Ingleside TX
dedupe.fingerprint: 1998-05-11|epa proposes rcra hazardous waste delisting for certain wastes at occidental chemical ingleside texas|us-tx-ingleside
